parameters.jsCode:
const payload = $('Extract Payload').first().json;
const postMortemsRaw = $('Fetch Post Mortems').first().json;
const contentPerfRaw = $('Fetch Content Performance').first().json;

const postMortems = Array.isArray(postMortemsRaw) ? postMortemsRaw : (postMortemsRaw?.data || []);
const contentPerf = Array.isArray(contentPerfRaw) ? contentPerfRaw : (contentPerfRaw?.data || []);

const opportunityData = payload.opportunityData || {};
const keywordVolumes = opportunityData.keywordVolumes || 'Not available';
const competitorInfo = opportunityData.competitorInfo || 'Not available';
const reviewComplaints = opportunityData.reviewComplaints || 'Not available';
const tamEstimate = opportunityData.tamEstimate || 'Not available';

const postMortemContext = postMortems.length > 0
  ? '

Previous post-mortems in this category (learn from these):
' + JSON.stringify(postMortems, null, 2)
  : '

No previous post-mortems in this category.';

const contentContext = contentPerf.length > 0
  ? '

Content performance data from similar businesses:
' + JSON.stringify(contentPerf, null, 2)
  : '

No content performance data from similar businesses.';

const prompt = 'You are the CEO of The Forge, an autonomous AI business operating system. Generate a comprehensive Product Requirements Document (PRD) for a new SaaS business.

## Business Information
- **Business Name**: ' + payload.businessName + '
- **Business ID**: ' + payload.businessId + '
- **Description**: ' + payload.businessDescription + '
- **Category**: ' + payload.category + '

## Market Intelligence
- **Keyword Volumes**: ' + (typeof keywordVolumes === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(keywordVolumes) : keywordVolumes) + '
- **Competitor Info**: ' + (typeof competitorInfo === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(competitorInfo) : competitorInfo) + '
- **Review Complaints (competitor weaknesses)**: ' + (typeof reviewComplaints === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(reviewComplaints) : reviewComplaints) + '
- **TAM Estimate**: ' + (typeof tamEstimate === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(tamEstimate) : tamEstimate) + postMortemContext + contentContext + '

## Your Task
Generate a COMPLETE PRD with ALL of the following sections. Be specific and detailed - this PRD will be used directly by Claude Code to build the entire product.

# ' + payload.businessName + ' - Product Requirements Document

## 1. Product Overview
- One-paragraph description
- Problem being solved
- Target market size

## 2. Target Persona
- Who they are (job title, business size, tech comfort)
- Daily workflow and pain points
- Current tools they use
- What would make them switch

## 3. Core Features (MVP)
For each feature:
- Feature name
- User story: "As a [persona], I want to [action] so that [benefit]"
- Acceptance criteria (3-5 bullet points)
- Priority: Must-have / Nice-to-have

## 4. Data Model
- Entity list with key fields
- Relationships between entities
- Example: customers (name, email, phone, vehicles[]), repair_orders (customer_id, status, line_items[], total), etc.

## 5. Pages & Routes
- Exact list: /, /login, /signup, /dashboard, /dashboard/[entity]/new, etc.
- What each page shows and does
- Navigation structure

## 6. Design System
- Primary color (hex) - chosen to match the vertical's aesthetic
- Secondary color
- Accent color
- Heading font (from Google Fonts)
- Body font
- Design aesthetic description (e.g., "clean, professional, trustworthy" or "bold, modern, energetic")

## 7. Pricing Tiers
- Tier 1: $99/mo - [features]
- Tier 2: $149/mo - [features]
- Tier 3: $199/mo - [features]
- 14-day free trial on all tiers
- What's gated behind each tier

## 8. Required Integrations
- Supabase Auth (email + password)
- Stripe Checkout (3 products/prices, 14-day trial)
- Stripe webhook -> forward to n8n at ${N8N_URL}/webhook/stripe-events
- Signup webhook -> POST to ${N8N_URL}/webhook/user-signup
- Contact form -> POST to ${N8N_URL}/webhook/support-ticket
- /demo route with interactive self-serve product tour (walkthrough of key features with screenshots/animations, ending with CTA to start free trial or book a call at cal.com/shalinp)
- Plausible analytics script (data-domain from env var)
- Live chat widget (Tawk.to or Crisp free tier) that routes messages to the n8n support webhook at ${N8N_URL}/webhook/support-ticket. The widget should appear on all pages as a floating button in the bottom-right corner.
- All n8n URLs via NEXT_PUBLIC_N8N_URL env var

## 9. SEO Plan
- 10 target keywords with estimated search volume
- 5 initial blog post titles targeting those keywords
- Meta title and description for the landing page
- Structured data (JSON-LD) for the business type

## 10. Competitive Weaknesses to Exploit
- Based on G2/Capterra 1-2 star reviews of competitors
- Specific UX issues, missing features, or pricing complaints
- How our product addresses each one

## 11. Content Quality Standards
BANNED WORDS (never use these): "delve", "leverage", "utilize", "harness", "elevate", "empower", "streamline", "robust", "seamless", "cutting-edge", "game-changer", "revolutionize", "transform", "innovative", "best-in-class", "world-class", "state-of-the-art", "paradigm", "synergy", "holistic"
BANNED PUNCTUATION: em dashes (use regular dashes instead)
Writing style: Direct, practical, conversational. Write like a real person talking to a friend who runs a business. No corporate speak.

## 12. Retention & Lock-In Mechanics (MANDATORY)
Every product MUST include these retention mechanics from Day 1. Churn below 2% monthly is critical for valuation.

### Data Lock-In
- The product must accumulate customer data that is painful to export (customer records, transaction history, documents, analytics, reports)
- Data should build value over time ("the more you use it, the more valuable your data becomes")
- Export should be possible (for trust) but importing elsewhere should be hard

### AI That Improves With Usage
- If this is an AI SaaS product, the AI must personalize to each customer over time
- After 3 months of usage, the AI should be noticeably better for that specific customer than a fresh install
- Examples: learns their terminology, remembers their preferences, adapts to their workflow patterns

### Integration Depth
- Must integrate with at least 2 tools the customer already uses (QuickBooks, Google Calendar, Stripe, Square, Mailchimp, etc.)
- The more connected the product, the harder to switch
- Each integration should sync bidirectionally

### System of Record
- The product should become WHERE the customer runs their daily operations
- Not a supplement to their workflow but the HUB of it
- Daily active usage should be the design goal, not weekly or monthly

### Switching Cost Design
- Custom templates/configurations that take time to set up
- Team member accounts and permissions
- Historical data and analytics that only exist in this product
- Trained AI models specific to their business

## 13. Launch Checklist
- [ ] All pages render on mobile
- [ ] Stripe checkout works end-to-end
- [ ] Signup fires n8n webhook
- [ ] Contact form posts to support webhook
- [ ] /demo has interactive product tour with trial CTA
- [ ] Plausible script loads
- [ ] 5 blog posts published
- [ ] Meta tags and OG image on all pages
- [ ] Sitemap.xml and robots.txt present
- [ ] Live chat widget loads on all pages
- [ ] Lighthouse performance > 50, accessibility > 80, SEO > 80

IMPORTANT: Output ONLY the PRD content. Do not wrap in code blocks. Start directly with the "# ' + payload.businessName + ' - Product Requirements Document" heading.';

return [{
  json: {
    ...payload,
    prdPrompt: prompt
  }
}];

parameters.text:
=You are the Comptroller of The Forge, an autonomous AI business operating system. Your job is to review PRDs before they go to build.

Review this PRD for the business "{{ $json.businessName }}" (category: {{ $json.category }}):

{{ $json.prdContent }}

## Your Review Criteria

1. **Revenue Ceiling**: Can this business plausibly reach $100K MRR within 2 years? Consider the pricing tiers, target market size, and conversion rates.

2. **Distribution Viability**: Are the target keywords actually rankable for a new site? Are there realistic distribution channels beyond SEO? Consider the competition level.

3. **Competitive Gap**: Is there a real opportunity here? Are the competitive weaknesses real and exploitable? Or is this a crowded market with no differentiation?

4. **PRD Quality**: Is the PRD specific enough for an AI to build the product? Are there missing sections, vague acceptance criteria, or unrealistic features?

5. **Integration Completeness**: Does the PRD include all required integrations (Supabase Auth, Stripe, n8n webhooks, Plausible, cal.com)?

## Output Format
Respond with EXACTLY this JSON format (no markdown, no code blocks, just raw JSON):
{"decision": "APPROVED or REJECTED", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "revenueCeiling": "your assessment", "distributionViability": "your assessment", "competitiveGap": "your assessment", "prdQuality": "your assessment", "integrationCompleteness": "your assessment", "notes": "any additional notes or concerns"}

Only REJECT if there is a fundamental problem. If the PRD is solid and the opportunity is real, APPROVE it.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ prd_status: 'approved', prd_generated_at: $now.toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'), prd_github_path: 'memory-bank/businesses/' + $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.businessId + '/prd.md' }) }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'approved', businessId: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.businessId, businessName: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.businessName, prdContent: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.prdContent, reviewDecision: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.reviewDecision, reviewConfidence: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.reviewConfidence, reviewNotes: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.reviewNotes }) }}

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ message: 'PRD REJECTED for ' + $json.businessName + '

Decision: ' + $json.reviewDecision + '
Confidence: ' + $json.reviewConfidence + '

Revenue Ceiling: ' + $json.reviewRevenueCeiling + '
Distribution: ' + $json.reviewDistribution + '
Competitive Gap: ' + $json.reviewCompetitiveGap + '

Notes: ' + $json.reviewNotes, priority: 'high' }) }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'rejected', businessId: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.businessId, businessName: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.businessName, reviewDecision: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.reviewDecision, reviewConfidence: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.reviewConfidence, reviewNotes: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.reviewNotes, reviewRevenueCeiling: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.reviewRevenueCeiling, reviewDistribution: $('Parse Comptroller Review').first().json.reviewDistribution }) }}
